title: The Sonnets CXXXIII - Beshrew that heart that makes my heart to groan
content:
Beshrew that heart that makes my heart to groan
    For that deep wound it gives my friend and me!
    Is�t not enough to torture me alone,
    But slave to slavery my sweet�st friend must be?
    Me from myself thy cruel eye hath taken,
    And my next self thou harder hast engross�d:
    Of him, myself, and thee I am forsaken;
    A torment thrice three-fold thus to be cross�d:
    Prison my heart in thy steel bosom�s ward,
    But then my friend�s heart let my poor heart bail;
    Whoe�er keeps me, let my heart be his guard;
    Thou canst not then use rigour in my jail:
    And yet thou wilt; for I, being pent in thee,
    Perforce am thine, and all that is in me.
meaning:
William Shakespeare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Sonnets CXXXIII - Beshrew that heart that makes my heart to groan"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
